outputs.description: Created by the system when the indicator is ingested. Generated GUID/unique identifier. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.action The action to apply if the indicator is matched within the targetProduct security tool. Possible values: "unknown", "allow", "block", or "alert".
outputs.name: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.id

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.additionalInformation A catchall area where extra data from the indicator not covered by the other indicator properties may be placed. Data placed into additionalInformation is typically not utilized by the targetProduct security tool. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.azureTenantId
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.confidence An integer representing confidence the indicator data accurately identifies malicious behavior. Possible values are 0 – 100, with 100 being the highest. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.description
outputs.name: Stamped by the system when the indicator is ingested. The Entra ID submitting client tenant ID.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.diamondModel The area of the Diamond Model in which this indicator exists. Possible values: "unknown", "adversary", "capability", "infrastructure", and "victim". MicrosoftATP.Indicators.domainName
outputs.name: Brief description (100 characters or less) of the threat represented by the indicator.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.expirationDateTime DateTime string indicating when the indicator expires. To avoid stale indicators persisting in the system, all indicators must have an expiration date. The timestamp type represents date and time information in ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 looks like: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' Date
outputs.name: Domain name associated with this indicator. Should be in the format subdomain.domain.topleveldomain.

outputs.description: An identification number that ties the indicator back to the indicator provider’s system (for example, a foreign key). MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileCompileDateTime DateTime the file was compiled. The timestamp type represents date and time information in ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 looks like: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z'
outputs.name: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.externalId

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileCreatedDateTime DateTime the file was created.The Timestamp type represents date and time information in ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 looks like: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' Date
outputs.name: Date

outputs.description: The type of hash stored in fileHashValue. Possible values: "unknown", "sha1", "sha256", "md5", "authenticodeHash256", "lsHash", and "ctph". MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileHashValue The file hash value.
outputs.name: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileHashType

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileMutexName The Mutex name used in file-based detections. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileName
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.filePacker The packer used to build the file in question. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.filePath
outputs.name: The name of the file if the indicator is file-based. Multiple file names may be delimited by commas.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileSize The size of the file in bytes. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.fileType
outputs.name: The path of the file indicating a compromise. Can be a Windows or *nix style path.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.ingestedDateTime The timestamp the indicator was ingested into the system. The Timestamp type represents date and time information in ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 looks like: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' Date
outputs.name: The text description of the type of file. For example, “Word Document” or “Binary”.

outputs.description: Used to deactivate indicators within the system. By default, any indicator submitted is set as active. However, providers may submit existing indicators with this set to ‘False’ to deactivate indicators in the system. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.knownFalsePositives Scenarios in which the indicator may cause false positives. This should be human-readable text.
outputs.name: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.isActive

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.lastReportedDateTime The last time the indicator was seen. The timestamp type represents date and time information in ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 looks like: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z' Date
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: The CIDR Block notation representation of the network referenced in this indicator. Use only if the source and destination cannot be identified. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkDestinationAsn The destination autonomous system identifier of the network referenced in the indicator.
outputs.name: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkCidrBlock

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkDestinationCidrBlock The CIDR Block notation representation of the destination network in this indicator. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkDestinationIPv4
outputs.name: Number

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkIPv4 The IPv4 IP address. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkIPv6
outputs.name: The TCP port destination.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkSourceAsn The source autonomous system identifier of the network referenced in the indicator. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkSourceCidrBlock
outputs.name: The decimal representation of the protocol field in the IPv4 header.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkSourceIPv4 The IPv4 IP address source. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkSourceIPv6
outputs.name: The CIDR Block notation representation of the source network in this indicator.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.networkSourcePort The TCP port source. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.passiveOnly
outputs.name: The IPv6 IP address source.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.severity An integer representing the severity of the malicious behavior identified by the data within the indicator. Possible values: "Informational", "Low", "MediumLow", "MediumHigh", and "High", where High is the most severe and Informational is not severe at all. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.targetProduct
outputs.name: Determines if the indicator should trigger an event that is visible to an end-user. When set to ‘true,’ security tools do not notify the end user that a ‘hit’ has occurred. This is most often treated as audit or silent mode by security products where they simply log that a match occurred but do not perform the action. Default value is false.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.threatType Each indicator must have a valid Indicator Threat Type. Possible values: "Botnet", "C2", "CryptoMining", "Darknet", "DDoS", "MaliciousUrl", "Malware", "Phishing", "Proxy", "PUA", and "WatchList". MicrosoftATP.Indicators.tlpLevel
outputs.name: A string representing a single security product to which the indicator should be applied.

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.url Uniform Resource Locator. This URL complies with RFC 1738. MicrosoftATP.Indicators.userAgent
outputs.name: Traffic Light Protocol value for the indicator. Possible values: "unknown", "white", "green", "amber", and "red".

outputs.description: MicrosoftATP.Indicators.vendorInformation Information about the vendor. File.Name
outputs.name: User-Agent string from a web request that could indicate compromise.

outputs.description: File.Size The size of the file in bytes. File.MD5
outputs.name: The full file name (including file extension).

outputs.description: File.SHA1 The SHA1 hash of the file. File.SHA256
outputs.name: The MD5 hash of the file.

outputs.description: File.SHA512 The SHA512 hash of the file. File.Type
outputs.name: The SHA256 hash of the file.

outputs.description: File.Path The path where the file is located. CrowdStrike.IOC.Type
outputs.name: The file type, as determined by libmagic (same as displayed in file entries).

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.Value The string representation of the indicator. CrowdStrike.IOC.ID
outputs.name: The type of the IOC.

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.Policy The policy of the indicator. CrowdStrike.IOC.Source
outputs.name: The full ID of the indicator (type:value).

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.ShareLevel The level at which the indicator will be shared. CrowdStrike.IOC.Expiration
outputs.name: The source of the IOC.

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.Description The description of the IOC. CrowdStrike.IOC.CreatedTime
outputs.name: The datetime the indicator will expire.

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.CreatedBy The identity of the user/process who created the IOC. CrowdStrike.IOC.ModifiedTime
outputs.name: The datetime the IOC was created.

outputs.description: CrowdStrike.IOC.ModifiedBy The identity of the user/process who last updated the IOC. Script Examples
outputs.name: The date and time the indicator was last modified.
